name: Store in variable
syntax: Store the value [Value] into the variable named [VariableName].
description: Creates a new variable or overwrites an existing variable with a specified piece of data.
example: Store the value 42 into the variable named "TotalScore".

name: Append to variable
syntax: Append the text [Value] to the end of the variable named [VariableName].
description: Adds a string or value directly to the conclusion of an existing variable's contents without clearing it.
example: Append the text ", thank you" to the end of the variable named "UserGreeting".

name: Prepend to variable
syntax: Prepend the text [Value] to the beginning of the variable named [VariableName].
description: Inserts a string or value directly at the very start of an existing variable's contents.
example: Prepend the text "Error: " to the beginning of the variable named "LogMessage".

name: Calculate addition
syntax: Add the number [Value] to the variable named [VariableName].
description: Performs basic mathematical addition, increasing the value stored within a target numeric variable.
example: Add the number 5 to the variable named "ItemCount".

name: Calculate subtraction
syntax: Subtract the number [Value] from the variable named [VariableName].
description: Performs basic mathematical subtraction, decreasing the value stored within a target numeric variable.
example: Subtract the number 10 from the variable named "RemainingBalance".

name: Calculate multiplication
syntax: Multiply the variable named [VariableName] by the number [Value].
description: Multiplies the current numeric contents of a variable by a specified factor.
example: Multiply the variable named "TaxAmount" by the number 1.2.

name: Calculate division
syntax: Divide the variable named [VariableName] by the number [Value].
description: Divides the current numeric contents of a variable by a specified divisor.
example: Divide the variable named "TotalCakeWeight" by the number 4.

name: Extract text chunk
syntax: Extract words [Start] through [End] of the text source [Source].
description: Isolates and returns a specific range of space-separated words out of a larger block of text.
example: Extract words 2 through 4 of the text source "The quick brown fox jumps".

name: Count items
syntax: Count the total number of items within the text source [Source].
description: Returns a numeric count of comma-separated components present within a given text string.
example: Count the total number of items within the text source "Flour, Sugar, Eggs, Butter".

name: Clear memory container
syntax: Delete the variable named [VariableName].
description: Completely purges a variable from memory, freeing up system resources.
example: Delete the variable named "TemporaryToken".

name: Create interface element
syntax: Create a new [ElementType] named [Name] on the current card.
description: Spawns a brand new user interface component (such as a button, field, or image) onto the active screen layout.
example: Create a new button named "CalculateTotal" on the current card.

name: Modify background color
syntax: Change the background color of the [ElementType] named [Name] to [Color].
description: Alters the interior solid fill color of a designated user interface object.
example: Change the background color of the field named "DisplayArea" to "blue".

name: Modify text color
syntax: Change the text color of the [ElementType] named [Name] to [Color].
description: Changes the display color of any text rendered inside or on top of the specified control element.
example: Change the text color of the button named "SubmitButton" to "white".

name: Relocate object
syntax: Move the [ElementType] named [Name] to the coordinates [X], [Y].
description: Repositions the physical center point of a user interface object to precise pixel coordinates on the screen.
example: Move the image named "LogoView" to the coordinates 150, 300.

name: Alter visibility state
syntax: Set the visibility of the [ElementType] named [Name] to [True/False].
description: Controls whether an object is rendered visibly on screen or hidden entirely from the user's view.
example: Set the visibility of the field named "ErrorMessage" to false.

name: Adjust text size
syntax: Change the text size of the [ElementType] named [Name] to [FontSize] points.
description: Dynamically scales the typographic size of text characters within an interface element.
example: Change the text size of the field named "MainHeader" to 24 points.

name: Go to another screen
syntax: Navigate to the card named [CardName].
description: Swaps the current screen view out to display a completely different design template or workflow canvas within the application.
example: Navigate to the card named "SettingsMenu".

name: Disable interface control
syntax: Deactivate the [ElementType] named [Name] to prevent user interaction.
livecode_equivalent: set the disabled of [ElementType] [Name] to true
description: Grays out an element and locks it so that mouse clicks, taps, or typing actions are completely ignored.
example: Deactivate the button named "PayNowButton" to prevent user interaction.

name: Enable interface control
syntax: Activate the [ElementType] named [Name] to allow user interaction.
livecode_equivalent: set the disabled of [ElementType] [Name] to false
description: Restores full interactive functionality to a previously locked or deactivated control element.
example: Activate the button named "PayNowButton" to allow user interaction.

name: Retrieve screen width
syntax: Obtain the pixel width of the current card.
description: Queries the layout engine to find the exact horizontal measurement of the active viewport.
example: Obtain the pixel width of the current card.
